title: A functional neuro-anatomical model of human attachment (NAMA): Insights from first- and second-person social neuroscience.

assets.abstract: Attachment theory, developed by Mary Ainsworth and John Bowlby about seventy years ago, has become one of the most influential and comprehensive contemporary psychology theories. It predicts that early social interactions with significant others shape the emergence of distinct self- and other-representations, the latter affecting how we initiate and maintain social relationships across the lifespan. A person's attachment history will therefore associate with inter-individual differences in emoti

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Intersubjectivity — second-person neuroscience, embodied empathy, shared intentionality, social predictive processing.
